parallel_group.prompt:
Implement the energy/redox/mass loop (EGC) detection utilities specified in agent-io/prds/find-flux-loops/fullprompt.md in this repo (KBUtilLib). Read that PRD in full first — its Implementation Decisions, the 'Confront resolutions (round 1)' block, and the numbered Acceptance Criteria are authoritative.

Add these methods to MSFBAUtils in src/kbutillib/ms_fba_utils.py:
1. build_full_model_from_template(template, index='0') — build a fresh cobra.Model with every template reaction via MSTemplateReaction.to_reaction(model, index), preserving template directionality; zero (lb=ub=0) any reaction whose id starts with EX_/DM_/SK_/bio; wrap as MSModelUtil and return.
2. add_probe_reaction(model, probe, compartment='c0') — generalize MSModelUtil.add_atp_hydrolysis: reuse an existing matching reaction via find_reaction(stoichiometry) (returns new=False) or create PROBE_<name>_<compartment> (lb=0, ub=1000) with seed.reaction annotation. Plus a module-level probe catalog keyed by group: atp (cpd00002+cpd00001->cpd00008+cpd00009+cpd00067), redox (balanced reduced->oxidized+H2(+cpd00067 as needed) per resolvable couple from the v1 list, gated behind enable_redox_probes=True, skip+warn unresolvable ids, no H2 sink), mass (irreversible sinks for exactly cpd00011/cpd00029/cpd00047/cpd00013), and all.
3. minimize_active_reactions(model, objective=None, active_filter=None, ...) — LP-max objective then LP-min total flux to get the active support (or accept active_filter); introduce ReactionUsePkg fu/ru binaries ONLY for active reactions via model.pkgmgr.getpkg('ReactionUsePkg').build_package(filter); minimize sum(fu+ru); return the count-minimal set with per-reaction reliability_score (assign_reliability_scores_to_reactions) and is_core. Cap the filter at the 500 largest-|flux| reactions and log if exceeded.
4. enumerate_alternative_reaction_sets(model, solution, ...) — generalize MSModelUtil.analyze_minimal_reaction_set: minimal-deviation objective = minimize sum(forward_var+reverse_var) over reactions at |v|<=1e-6; knock out each active reaction (lb=0 if forward-used, ub=0 if reverse-used) and report per reaction its alternatives, coupled set, and essential flag (knockout->infeasible).
5. find_flux_loops(template, objective='all', compartment='c0', max_loops_per_probe=5, ...) — orchestrator: build closed model; for each probe in the resolved group (or a custom cobra.Reaction): add probe, LP-maximize (vmax<=1e-6 => no loop), pin probe, LP-min flux, minimize_active_reactions on the support, enumerate_alternative_reaction_sets, strip ReactionUse vars, integer-cut block + re-maximize up to max_loops_per_probe. Return the frozen-key dict-keyed-by-probe schema (no extra keys). After return the model must be byte-for-byte equivalent to its pre-call state (probes removed, no fu_/ru_ vars/constraints, all bounds restored from a pre-run snapshot).

Thresholds: active iff |flux|>1e-6; zero iff |flux|<=1e-9; pin fraction_of_optimum default 1.0; flux_min_threshold default 1e-6.

Generalize (do not copy) these prior-art functions: MSModelUtil.add_atp_hydrolysis (msmodelutl.py), binary_check_gapfilling_solution + ReactionUsePkg (gapfillingpkg.py / reactionusepkg.py), MSModelUtil.analyze_minimal_reaction_set (msmodelutl.py), and the iterative-block pattern in MSFBAUtils.unblock_objective_with_exchanges (ms_fba_utils.py). Match existing ms_fba_utils style and logging (log_info). Do NOT hard-pin a ModelSEEDpy version in pyproject; document the relied-on APIs in a module docstring.

Add tests using small hand-built cobra.Model stand-ins wrapped via MSModelUtil.get (NOT synthetic MSTemplate fixtures): (a) find_flux_loops end-to-end reports exactly a planted ATP futile cycle and an empty list once the bad reversibility is corrected; (b) minimize_active_reactions returns the count-minimal (short) path on a short-vs-long parallel-path toy; (c) enumerate_alternative_reaction_sets reports two interchangeable reactions as each other's alternative and flags the essential reaction; (d) add_probe_reaction adds correct stoichiometry/annotation and reuses an existing match rather than duplicating; (e) the model is unmodified after a find_flux_loops run. Adopt the non-binding refinements (loop dedup by direction-set hash, perf logging, clean-noise specificity test) if cheap.
parallel_group.success_criteria: MSFBAUtils in src/kbutillib/ms_fba_utils.py gains find_flux_loops, build_full_model_from_template, add_probe_reaction, minimize_active_reactions, and enumerate_alternative_reaction_sets implementing the PRD; the new pytest suite passes under the repo's standard test command, including: find_flux_loops reports exactly the planted ATP-cycle EGC on the stand-in fixture and an empty list when the reversibility is corrected; minimize_active_reactions returns the count-minimal path on the short-vs-long toy; enumerate_alternative_reaction_sets reports interchangeable reactions as alternatives and flags the essential one; add_probe_reaction reuses an existing matching reaction instead of duplicating; and the input model is unmodified after a find_flux_loops run (no probe reactions, no fu_/ru_ variables/constraints, bounds restored).
